data.title: A whale withdrew 4,000 WBETH from Binance, equivalent to about 8 million US dollars
data.description: Odaily Planet Daily News According to Onchain Lens monitoring, a whale withdrew 4,000 WBETH (about 8 million US dollars) from Binance. Previously, the whale withdrew 18 million S (about 9.67 million US dollars) and 1.6 million CAKE (about 3.97 million US dollars) for staking, which will be locked until March 12, 2026.

data.title: Castle Island Ventures partner: L2 is too greedy, leading to Ethereum's sluggish performance
data.description: Odaily Planet Daily News The ETH/BTC exchange rate has hit its lowest level in nearly five years, and some investors even call ETH a "completely dead" investment. Nic Carter, partner of Castle Island Ventures, believes that "ETH died in its own hands" because L2 was too greedy, causing Ethereum transaction activity, user growth and fees/revenue to decline. Ethereum can be seen as a network with practicality, but it is "definitely not" an investment. (Cointelegraph)

data.title: CoinFund: Global stablecoin supply could increase to $1 trillion, which could be a catalyst for the bull market peak
data.description:
Odaily Planet Daily News CoinFund Managing Partner David Pakman said that the global stablecoin supply could surge from the current scale of about US$225 billion to US$1 trillion, which could then become a key catalyst for the growth of the cryptocurrency market. 
David Pakman added that this scale of growth is actually not large compared to the global financial market. Since 2021, the volume of stablecoin transactions has increased by more than 22 times, indicating an increase in on-chain capital flows, and the current stablecoin supply can only indicate that the market may still be in the middle of the cycle and has not yet reached the peak of the bull market. (Cointelegrah)
